title: Live Aluminum Prices Today
summary: Aluminum prices advanced 0.85% today, reaching $3,277.86 per ton globally and ¥22,656 in China, with a later update showing 0.17% rise to $3,425.83/ton globally and ¥23,605/ton in China. The upward momentum stems from geopolitical shockwaves due to Iranian strikes on major Middle East smelters like Emirates Global Aluminium and Aluminium Bahrain, plus the Strait of Hormuz closure, threatening 9% of global primary aluminum supply. This supply bottleneck supports elevated prices amid a structural deficit.

title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Aluminum fell to 3,429.55 USD/T on March 31, 2026, down 0.06% from the previous day, though it rose to 3,461.95 USD/T up 0.89% in another update. UK aluminum futures gained 12% in March, the strongest since 2018, amid supply threats from Iranian strikes damaging plants operated by Aluminium Bahrain and Emirates Global Aluminium. Over the past month, prices rose 7.36% to 8.37%, up 36.94% to 38.23% year-over-year.

title: Aluminum Prices Jump as Iran Hit Middle East Producers
summary: Aluminum prices increased 5.27% to 3447.88 USD/T on March 30, 2026. The jump is attributed to Iranian strikes impacting Middle East aluminum producers. This contributes to recent market volatility and upward trends.

title: Steel fell to 3,124 CNY/T on March 31, 2026
summary: Steel rebar futures declined 0.64% to 3,124 CNY/T as investors assessed supply constraints linked to the Iran war, which have elevated fuel and shipping costs. Israel's strikes on two major Iranian steel factories tied to the IRGC have added further complexity to the global steel market.

title: HRC Steel rose to 1,063.94 USD/T on March 31, 2026
summary: Hot-Rolled Coil Steel increased 0.09% to 1,063.94 USD/T, reaching its highest level since January 2024. Over the past month, HRC Steel gained 4.72%, and is up 18.88% compared to the same period last year.

title: Nucor increases consumer spot price for hot-rolled coil
summary: Nucor has raised its consumer spot price for hot-rolled coil to $1,035 per short ton, a $10/st increase from the previous week. Sheet and plate prices have remained flat or modestly higher, continuing a trend observed since the beginning of Q4.

title: UK HRC assessment jumps by £140/t in March
summary: Argus' weekly UK HRC assessment increased by £140/t throughout March, reaching £675/t on March 26. Hot-rolled coil sheet prices have risen from approximately £550/t to as high as £850/t over the same period.

title: U.S. Steel Manufacturers Plan Near-Term Capacity Additions
summary: U.S. steel manufacturers are planning more than $6 billion in capital-spending projects set to begin in Q2 2026, with major projects in Texas and Louisiana. United States Steel (now owned by Nippon Steel) is adding a premium thread line at its Fairfield, Alabama facility and preparing upgrades at its Mon Valley Works plant in Pennsylvania. Hyundai Steel Company is making progress on a new 2.7 million ton per year steel mill in Modeste, Louisiana using direct-reduction technology.

title: US raw steel production increases by 1.2 percent - week 13, 2026
summary: U.S. raw steel production increased 1.2 percent in the week ending March 28, 2026, reaching 1,803,000 net tons with a capability utilization rate of 77.9 percent. This represents a 5.9 percent increase compared to the same week in the previous year, and year-to-date production through March 28, 2026 was up 5.0 percent from the prior year.

title: U.S. Steel confirms restart of Granite City B blast furnace
summary: U.S. Steel has confirmed the restart of the B blast furnace at its Granite City Works facility near St. Louis, signaling continued capacity expansion efforts.

title: Tata Steel Unveils Scrap-Based EAF Facility
summary: Tata Steel announced it has started up its new scrap-based electric arc furnace (EAF) facility in Hi-Tech Valley, Ludhiana, India. This innovation promotes sustainability by utilizing scrap metal, reducing reliance on primary raw materials and lowering carbon emissions in steel production. The facility represents a step towards greener steelmaking technologies.

title: Tata Steel Teams With University of Science and Technology Beijing for Green Steel Research
summary: Tata Steel signed a memorandum of understanding with the University of Science and Technology Beijing (USTB) to advance green steelmaking technologies. The collaboration focuses on research into sustainable steel production methods. This partnership aims to innovate in reducing environmental impact through technological advancements.

title: Copper held steady near $5.5 per pound on Tuesday but remained on track to lose nearly 10% in March
summary: Copper held steady near $5.5 per pound on Tuesday but is on track for a nearly 10% loss in March, its worst monthly performance since July last year. Industrial metals face pressure from Middle East conflict disruptions and surging energy prices, raising inflation and slowing global activity concerns. Copper rose to 5.55 USD/Lbs on March 31, up 1.30% from the previous day.

title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco’s smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday after a collapse. This development could impact copper supply from one of the world's largest producers. The restart follows an unspecified incident at the facility.

title: Rio Tinto's Nuton technology produces first copper
summary: Rio Tinto has produced the first copper cathode using its proprietary Nuton bioleaching technology at Gunnison Copper’s Johnson Camp mine, achieving the lowest mine-to-metal carbon footprint in the U.S. at 0.82 kg CO₂-e per kg copper. The technology, developed over 30 years, enables cleaner, faster, and more efficient production, with 100% renewable energy matching via certificates. It targets 30,000 tonnes of refined copper over a four-year demonstration and supports the U.S. domestic supply chain.

title: Sustainable and efficient operations for copper miners
summary: Copper miners must adopt sustainable and efficient operations amid growing clean energy demand and supply deficits, with exploration budgets at a nine-year high. Innovations like digitalization, electrification, and low-carbon products—such as Boliden's copper under 1.5 kg CO₂/kg—are key to navigating regulatory challenges and reducing environmental impact. The sector faces low-grade deposits, requiring economically and environmentally friendly extraction methods.

title: Innovations to close the copper supply-demand gap
summary: Copper demand is projected to grow from 26 Mt in 2024 to 35 Mt by 2035 due to renewables, EVs, and data centers, facing a 10 Mt shortfall without innovation. Startups are developing technologies for primary sulfide ores, energy-efficient mining, and reduced environmental impacts like water usage. Novel grinding technologies and other advancements aim to bridge the gap with $250 billion in required capital.

title: Rewiring a sustainable, clean energy future with copper
summary: Copper's recyclability—nearly one-third of global demand met through recycling and 80% of ever-mined copper still in use—supports sustainability in the energy transition. Demand could double by 2050 for EVs and renewables, with mining adopting responsible practices to reduce water, energy use, and emissions. Technological innovation and circular economy approaches minimize new mining needs.

title: ASU works with Freeport-McMoRan to strengthen supply of a critical mineral — copper
summary: ASU partners with Freeport-McMoRan to develop AI, robotics, and sensor technologies for more efficient, sustainable domestic copper mining amid vulnerable supply chains. The project automates ore classification to improve processing speed and precision, addressing electrification-driven demand. Freeport leads in innovative techniques for safe, sustainable copper production.

title: Nickel Mines 2025 presentation: resilience amid downturn, growth ahead
summary: Nickel Mines reported total nickel production of 133,000 tonnes in 2025, with projections to reach 167,000 tonnes in 2026. The company achieved record mine ore sales of 9.9 million wet metric tonnes, expecting 14.3 million wmt in 2026 after quota approval. Processing operations outperformed capacities, including HPAL producing 85,031 tonnes of nickel, 42% above design.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's biggest nickel mine in Indonesia, has been ordered to cut ore production quota to 12 million tons this year from 42 million tons in 2025 to boost global prices. The company plans to apply for a revision. This follows Indonesia's signals to reduce mine output, impacting nickel used in stainless steel and EV batteries.

title: Nickel Headed to $20,000 as Indonesia Limits Production, EV Demand Surges
summary: Indonesia announced a 2026 nickel ore production target of 250-260 million tons, flat-to-down from 2025, supporting nickel prices toward $20,000 per ton amid robust EV demand growth of 20%. Analysts emphasize the need for production discipline verification. Canada Nickel advances its Crawford project toward 2026 construction amid emerging supply deficits.

title: Canada's Nickel Processing Innovation for Clean Energy
summary: Canada is advancing nickel processing technology with innovations like advanced leaching chemistry, closed-loop water systems, and energy efficiency improvements in electrowinning to support clean energy applications. These efforts emphasize sustainability through renewable energy integration, waste heat recovery, and potential net-negative carbon emissions via carbon capture and mineral carbonation of tailings. High-purity Class 1 nickel enables 20-25% better energy density in EV batteries, commanding a 15-25% price premium.
